content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To    :File                                                              Date  :April 5, 1983




From  : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Ted

Subject: Conversation with Vance Raye on March 31, 1983


       Vance Raye counsel to the Governor, called to ask how a
season pass to all major league baseball games should be
disclosed by the Governor.  I advised him that he should find
out the value of season tickets to an individual team's ball
games and add on some amount for the fact that this pass
admitted one to any major league ball game anywhere in the
country.

       Vance indicated that this sounded like more trouble than
the pass was worth so it would probably would be returned.

sections.question_synthetic: How should a season pass to all major league baseball games be valued and disclosed by the Governor?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that the value should be determined by finding the cost of season tickets to an individual team's games and adding an amount for the pass's ability to admit the holder to any major league game anywhere in the country.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Internal FPPC memorandum rather than formal advice letter; no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections; document appears incomplete (ends mid-sentence with 'it~'). Classified as correspondence and FPPC response based on staff providing advisory guidance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should a season pass to all major league baseball games be valued and disclosed by the Governor?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advised that the value should be determined by finding the cost of season tickets to an individual team's games and adding an amount for the pass's ability to admit the holder to any major league game anywhere in the country.

I advised him that he should find out the value of season tickets to an individual team's ball games and add on some amount for the fact that this pass admitted one to any major league ball game anywhere in the country. Vance indicated that this sounded like more trouble than the pass was worth so it would probably would be returned.
embedding.summary: A memorandum documenting a phone conversation between FPPC staff and Vance Ray, counsel to the Governor, regarding the disclosure value of a season pass to all major league baseball games.
